steps.text: Submit the "Procurement Request Package" to the Office of Financial Resources (OFR).

constraints.text: Comply with Executive Order 13990, Protecting Public Health and the Environment and Restoring Science To Tackle the Climate Crisis.
Comply with food safety standards in the U.S. Food Code.

steps.text: Include general requirements and guidance in all procurement request packages, RFPs, and new or renegotiated contracts and/or permits to ensure vendors are selected that can adopt and implement healthy food service guidelines.

Draft Scope of Work language to emphasize the purpose of the Food Service Guidelines and indicate that CDC is requesting proposals from experienced food service vendors with the capacity to offer affordable healthier options, maintain environmentally responsible practices, follow food safety procedures, and use behavioral design.

Incorporate Food and Nutrition Standards (Prepared foods, Packaged foods, and Beverages) at the standard implementation level into the procurement request package, RFP Statement of Work, and contractual/permit performance requirements.

Require the vendor to implement at least one standard from the behavioral design categories (Placement and Layout, Product Innovations and Default, Pricing and Promotion, Tableware, Information, Organizational Policy) at the innovative implementation level.
Require the vendor to comply with the standards included in each of the facility efficiency, environmental support, and community development categories at the standard implementation level.

constraints.text: Follow food safety procedures to minimize the risk of foodborne illnesses.

steps.text: Require the vendor to comply with the specified food safety standards at the standard level of implementation.
Explicitly require adherence to all applicable state and/or local food safety regulations in the final contract.
Require the vendor to produce a reporting plan with monthly sales data and monthly or weekly operating statements in the RFP and subsequent approved contract or permit.
Ensure the selected food service vendor meets all minimum performance requirements.

constraints.text: Vendors that indicate an ability to implement at the innovative level may be given preference.
The reporting plan must describe how the vendor will monitor implementation efforts, outline tracking methods for healthier items, and include key indicators reported to the COR or project officer.
CDC reserves the right to establish a performance improvement plan with time-limited, incremental targets if the vendor needs to demonstrate progress.

steps.text: Develop a Procurement Request Package, with a Statement of Work, that reflects minimum requirements defined by this policy.
Process the Procurement Request Package submitted by the Program Official and take all actions on behalf of the U.S. Government as permitted by the Federal Acquisition Regulations.
Determine, post award, the adequacy of performance by the vendor in accordance with the terms and conditions of this policy and stipulated in the contract and/or permit.
Ensure compliance with requirements through periodic reviews and inspections.
Assist the vendor with the resolution of any issues that arise regarding performance or implementation.

Assist the Program Official, CO, or assigned COR or Project Officer in integrating Food Service Guidelines requirements, as defined by this policy, into Procurement Request Packages, CDC-issued RFPs, and final approved contracts or permits.
Provide technical direction and guidance related to issues that may jeopardize the successful implementation of this policy.

preconditions.text: The contract and/or permit has been awarded.

constraints.text: Actions must be permitted by the Federal Acquisition Regulations.
Adequacy of performance must be determined in accordance with the terms and conditions of this policy and stipulated in the contract and/or permit.
